description_long: This object is a lush clump of flowering plants with long, slender, grass-like green leaves radiating outward from the base. Several upright stems rise from the center, each topped with a daisy-like flower featuring a prominent dark central disc and numerous elongated, pinkish-red petals arranged in a radial pattern. The flowers are distributed at varying heights, creating a layered, natural look typical of ornamental garden species such as Echinacea or coneflower. The foliage is dense and spiky, with leaves that are linear to lanceolate in shape, contributing to a wild, untamed appearance. The overall structure is bushy, with the tallest flower standing well above the rest, and the plant appears well-suited for placement in urban parks, roadside plantings, or landscaped medians. The coloration is dominated by vibrant greens and pinkish-reds, with subtle shading and textural detail on both leaves and petals.
description: A bushy cluster of flowering plants with long green leaves and several tall stems topped by pinkish-red, daisy-like flowers. The flowers have a dark central disc and radiating petals, creating a vibrant, ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the dense foliage and the prominent front-facing flower, with several stems and flowers visible at different heights.
description_view_1: From this angle, the spread of the leaves is more pronounced, and the flowers appear more clustered, with the tallest flower clearly visible at the back.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the elongated leaves extending outward and the staggered arrangement of the flowers, with the tallest flower slightly off-center.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the tallest flower most prominently, with the foliage forming a dense base and the flowers arranged in a layered, vertical fashion.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, daisy-like configuration around a central disc, with each flower displaying a single whorl of elongated, evenly spaced petals. The arrangement is symmetrical and the petals slightly overlap at their bases.
